payload.title: Polymarket CTF Exchange V2 API — cutover April 28 ~11:00 UTC
payload.summary: Polymarket is deploying a full exchange overhaul tomorrow. Three breaking changes: (1) EIP-712 domain version must change from '1' to '2' — v1-signed orders rejected at cutover. (2) Pagination endpoints replaced by keyset cursors. (3) POST /submit no longer returns transactionHash immediately. market-builder-agent templates must be audited before the trading-swarm service is ever started. System is not yet live — no production incident. Migration guide: https://docs.polymarket.com/v2-migration

payload.title: DeepSeek V4-Flash local inference may fit UM890 Pro — new sovereignty angle
payload.summary: V4-Flash Q2 GGUF requires ~71GB RAM (MoE 284B × 2bit/8 = 71GB minimum). UM890 Pro has 96GB DDR5 with ~79GB headroom after existing Gemma models (17GB). Prior decision 2026-05-02 blocked the Chinese-hosted API on sovereignty grounds. Local inference via downloaded MIT-licensed weights is a distinct question — data never leaves the server, weights hosted on US HuggingFace. model-routing.md notes MiniMax M2.7 open weights as clearing sovereignty blocker as precedent. If local inference approved: Tier 3 tasks (quant-research, backtest) move to zero marginal cost at ~15 tok/s. V4-Flash achieves 79.0% SWE-bench Verified vs Sonnet 79.6% — comparable capability.
